practicals.title: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
practicals.details: Micro-chemical methods available wherever possible.

practicals.title: Bending a glass tube
practicals.details: Micro-chemical methods available wherever possible.

practicals.title: Drawing out a glass jet
practicals.details: Micro-chemical methods available wherever possible.

practicals.title: Boring a cork
practicals.details: Micro-chemical methods available wherever possible.

practicals.title: Determination of melting point of an organic compound
practicals.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

practicals.title: Determination of boiling point of an organic compound
practicals.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

practicals.title: Crystallization (Alum/Copper Sulphate/Benzoic Acid)
practicals.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
practicals.details: Crystallization of impure sample.

practicals.title: Determination of pH using fruit juices/indicators
practicals.details: Using pH paper or universal indicator.

practicals.title: Comparing pH of strong and weak acids
Study pH change in titration using universal indicator
Study pH change by common-ion effect
practicals.details: In case of weak acids and weak bases.

practicals.title: Shift in equilibrium (Ferric ions/thiocyanate)
practicals.details: Study shift by increasing/decreasing concentration.

practicals.title: Shift in equilibrium ([Co(H2O)6]2+/chloride ions)
practicals.details: Study shift by changing concentration of either ion.

practicals.title: Preparation of standard solution (Mechanical/Electronic balance)
Standard Oxalic acid preparation and NaOH determination
practicals.details: Titration against standard solution.

practicals.title: Standard Sodium carbonate preparation and HCl determination
practicals.details: Titration against standard Sodium Carbonate solution.

practicals.title: Determination of one anion and one cation (Salt Analysis)
practicals.details: Mandatory: NH4+ as cation. Insoluble salts excluded.

practicals.title: Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
Preparation of lyophilic and lyophobic sols (Surface Chemistry)
practicals.details: Lyophilic: starch/egg albumin/gum. Lyophobic: Al(OH)3/Ferric hydroxide.

practicals.title: Dialysis of sol prepared in Surface Chemistry experiment
Study role of emulsifying agents
practicals.details: Stabilizing the emulsion of different oils.

practicals.title: Effect of concentration and temperature on reaction rate (Kinetics)
practicals.details: Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid.

practicals.title: Study reaction rates: Iodide ion with H2O2 or Clock Reaction
practicals.details: Using starch solution as indicator.

practicals.title: Enthalpy of dissolution (Thermochemistry)
practicals.details: Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate.

practicals.title: Enthalpy of neutralization (Strong acid/base)
practicals.details: HCl and NaOH.

practicals.title: Determination of enthalpy change (Hydrogen bond formation)
practicals.details: Between Acetone and Chloroform.

practicals.title: Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu
practicals.details: With change in concentration of electrolytes at room temperature.

practicals.title: Separation of pigments by paper chromatography (Rf values)
practicals.details: Extracts from leaves and flowers.

practicals.title: Preparation of double salt or Potash Alum
Separation of constituents in inorganic mixture (Chromatography)
practicals.details: Two cations with large difference in Rf values.

practicals.title: Preparation of Organic Compounds (Acetanilide/Di-benzal acetone/p-Nitroacetanilide/Aniline yellow)
Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, Alcoholic, Phenolic, etc.)
practicals.details: Aldehydic, Ketonic, Carboxylic and Amino groups.

practicals.title: Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins
practicals.details: In pure samples and detection in given foodstuffs.

practicals.title: Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution
practicals.details: Titration against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate.

practicals.title: Determination of one anion and one cation (Class XII Salt Analysis)
practicals.details: Mandatory: NH4+ as cation. Insoluble salts excluded.

projects.title: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water (sulphide ion)
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Methods of purification of water
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Testing hardness, Iron, Fluoride, Chloride in regional drinking water
projects.details: Study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any).

projects.title: Foaming capacity of washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
projects.details: Investigation of foaming capacity of different washing soaps.

projects.title: Acidity of tea leaves samples
projects.details: Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves.

projects.title: Rate of evaporation of different liquids
projects.details: Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids.

projects.title: Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers
projects.details: Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers.

projects.title: Acidity of fruit/vegetable juices
projects.details: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices.

unit_breakdown.topic: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences... Present and defend opinions...

internal_assessment.policy: 33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.
internal_assessment.chapter_weightage_policy: No chapter wise weightage is provided; care to cover all chapters.

mandatory_experiments_section_b.task: Quantitative analysis (Preparation of standard solution and titration)
details: KMn4 determination using Oxalic acid or Mohr's salt
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (NH4+ as mandatory cation)

section_a_experiments.task: Tests for functional groups (Alcoholic, Carboxylic, Aldehyde, Ketonic)
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates and proteins

project_assessment.nature: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_topics_class_xi: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water (sulphide ion)
Methods of purification of water
Testing hardness, Iron, Fluoride, Chloride in regional drinking water
Foaming capacity of washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
Acidity of tea leaves samples
Rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers
Acidity of fruit/vegetable juices
suggested_topics_class_xii: Presence of oxalate ions in guava at ripening stages
Quantity of casein in milk samples
Soybean milk preparation and comparison with natural milk
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase (pH/temp effect)
Rate of fermentation: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi
Study of common food adulterants

competency_groups.title: Fundamental Chemical Concepts and Quantitative Analysis
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the nature of matter, measurement principles, atomic theory, and stoichiometric calculations.
competencies: Understand the importance and nature of Chemistry as a discipline.
Represent chemical phenomena at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels.
Apply laws of conservation of mass in chemical combinations.
Utilize Dalton's Atomic Theory to explain atomic masses.
Perform stoichiometric calculations involving mole concept and molar masses.
Calculate percentage composition and apply quantitative analysis techniques.

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering the discovery of sub-atomic particles, evolution of atomic models, and quantum mechanical concepts.
competencies: Trace the historical development from Rutherford to Bohr's model for hydrogen atom.
Understand the transition towards the Quantum Mechanical Model of the Atom.
Apply principles of wave-particle duality in explaining atomic spectra.

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on the logic behind element classification, periodic law, and trends in elemental properties.
competencies: Justify the need for classifying elements based on recurring patterns.
Apply Modern Periodic Law to determine electronic configurations of s-, p-, d-, f-blocks.
Analyze periodic trends including atomic radius, ionization enthalpy, and electronegativity.
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories and Molecular Geometry
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving various theories of chemical bonding, hybridization, and molecular structure prediction.
competencies: Differentiate between Ionic (Electrovalent) and Covalent bonds using Kossel-Lewis approach.
Predict molecular geometry using VSEPR Theory based on electron pair repulsion.
Explain bonding in molecules using Valence Bond Theory, Hybridisation, and Molecular Orbital Theory.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamic Principles and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to energy changes in chemical reactions, calorimetry, and criteria for spontaneity.
competencies: Measure enthalpy (∆H) and internal energy (∆U) using calorimetric techniques.
Distinguish between different types of reaction enthalpies (formation, combustion, etc.).
Apply Gibbs Energy criteria to determine the spontaneity of chemical reactions.

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium Dynamics and Ionic Systems
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering dynamic equilibrium, Le Chatelier's principle, and ionic equilibria in solutions.
competencies: Distinguish between physical and chemical equilibria as dynamic processes.
Apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium to calculate Kc and Kp values.
Predict shifts in equilibrium position using Le Chatelier's Principle.

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the identification of oxidation-reduction processes, balancing equations, and electrode applications.
competencies: Identify oxidizing and reducing agents using classical ideas and electron transfer concepts.
Assign oxidation numbers to elements in compounds and ions.

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering carbon tetravalence, nomenclature, isomerism, reaction mechanisms, and purification techniques.
competencies: Apply IUPAC rules for the classification and naming of organic compounds.
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the structure, properties, and reactions of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic compounds.
competencies: Classify hydrocarbons based on saturation (saturated vs unsaturated) and ring structures.

competency_groups.title: Laboratory Safety and Basic Techniques
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the safe handling of glassware, basic manipulations, and preparation techniques.
competencies: Execute micro-chemical methods for cutting, bending, and drawing glass tubes.

competency_groups.title: Characterization and Purification Methods
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on determining physical constants (melting/boiling points) and purification techniques like crystallization.
competencies: Determine melting and boiling points of organic compounds.
competency_groups.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
competency_groups.chapter_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

competency_groups.title: pH Analysis and Ionic Equilibrium Applications
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to measuring pH, understanding buffer solutions, and studying ionic equilibria.
competencies: Determine the pH of various solutions using indicators or paper.

competency_groups.title: Volumetric Analysis and Titration Skills
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the preparation of standard solutions and performing titrations for quantitative estimation.
competencies: Prepare standard solutions using precise weighing techniques.

competency_groups.title: Qualitative Salt Analysis and Ion Detection
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on identifying cations and anions in unknown salt samples through systematic chemical tests.
competencies: Identify specific cations (e.g., Pb2+, Cu2+) and anions (e.g., CO3 2-, Cl-) using characteristic reactions.

competency_groups.title: Content-Based Experimental Investigation
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the design and execution of experiments linked to theoretical concepts.
competencies: Conduct content-based experiments linking theory with practical observation.

competency_groups.title: Scientific Inquiry and Project Work
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to conducting scientific investigations, data collection, and reporting on chemical phenomena.
competencies: Design and execute a project involving laboratory testing or information gathering.

competency_groups.title: Communication and Record Keeping
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the maintenance of accurate records and oral defense of practical work.
competencies: Maintain a comprehensive class record of experiments.

competency_groups.title: Solution Chemistry and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering types of solutions, concentration expressions, vapor pressure laws, and molar mass determination.
competencies: Calculate concentrations using various expression methods.

competency_groups.title: Electrochemical Cells and Applications
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving galvanic cells, Nernst equation applications, conductance, and corrosion processes.
competencies: Analyze the functioning of Galvanic and Electrolytic cells.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to factors affecting reaction rates, rate laws, collision theory, and temperature dependence.
competencies: Determine the order of reactions using integrated rate equations.

competency_groups.title: d- and f-Block Element Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering electronic configurations, general properties of transition elements, and lanthanoid/actinoid chemistry.
competencies: Correlate position in the periodic table with electronic configuration.

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry and Bonding Models
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving Werner's theory, nomenclature rules, isomerism types, and bonding theories in complexes.
competencies: Apply IUPAC nomenclature to coordination compounds.

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Competencies focusing on nomenclature, nature of C-X bonds, preparation methods, and substitution reactions.
competencies: Predict chemical reactivity based on the nature of the carbon-halogen bond.

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols, and Ethers Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering classification, nomenclature, functional group structures, and commercial applications.
competencies: Distinguish between alcohols, phenols, and ethers based on structure.

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Compounds: Aldehydes and Ketones
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the structure of carbonyl groups, preparation methods, and characteristic reactions.
competencies: Differentiate between aldehydes and ketones based on functional group position.

competency_groups.title: Amines: Structure and Diazonium Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering amine classification, preparation methods, and the synthesis of aromatic compounds via diazonium salts.
competencies: Synthesize aromatic compounds using diazonium salt reactions.

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules and Biological Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the structure, classification, and functions of carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, and nucleic acids.
competencies: Identify major classes of biomolecules including carbohydrates and proteins.

unit_breakdown.topic: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences... Present and defend opinions...

internal_assessment.policy: 33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.
internal_assessment.chapter_weightage_policy: No chapter wise weightage is provided; care to cover all chapters.

mandatory_experiments_section_b.task: Quantitative analysis (Preparation of standard solution and titration)
details: KMn4 determination using Oxalic acid or Mohr's salt
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (NH4+ as mandatory cation)

section_a_experiments.task: Tests for functional groups (Alcoholic, Carboxylic, Aldehyde, Ketonic)
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates and proteins

project_assessment.nature: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_topics_class_xi: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water (sulphide ion)
Methods of purification of water
Testing hardness, Iron, Fluoride, Chloride in regional drinking water
Foaming capacity of washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
Acidity of tea leaves samples
Rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers
Acidity of fruit/vegetable juices
suggested_topics_class_xii: Presence of oxalate ions in guava at ripening stages
Quantity of casein in milk samples
Soybean milk preparation and comparison with natural milk
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase (pH/temp effect)
Rate of fermentation: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi
Study of common food adulterants
